content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 9, 1988

Linda D. Hamilton
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk
Superior Court/County Clerk
County of San Bernardino
351 N. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor
San Bernardino, CA 92415-0210

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-141

Dear Ms. Hamilton:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding the filing of statements of economic interest by
nonincumbent judicial candidates pursuant to the provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

Where are nonincumbent candidates for the office of judge
required to file their statements of economic interest?

CONCLUSION

Nonincumbent candidates for the office of judge should file
their statements of economic interest with the clerk of the
court. The clerk shall make and retain a copy and forward the
original to the Commission, which shall be the filing officer.

ANALYSIS

The Act requires that persons who hold public office, and
candidates for such offices, file statements of economic
interests (SEIs) disclosing investments and interests in real
property. (Sections 87200 and 87201.) The office of judge is
specifically covered by these provisions of the Act.

_______________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Linda D. Hamilton
May 9, 1988
Page 2


     Section 87500 et seq. sets forth where these documents are
to be filed.  Section 87500(i) directs judges to file their
statements with the clerk of court, who is then required to
forward the original to the Commission and retain a copy.
However, the language of this provision does not include
candidates for the office of judge.  Thus, while it is clear
that nonincumbent judicial candidates must file SEIs, it is not
clear where they must be filed.

     Section 87500(m) applies to persons not otherwise provided
for in Section 87500, but relates to individuals identified in
conflict of interest codes as designated employees with
reporting obligations.  It simply requires compliance with the
provisions of the agency's conflict of interest code.  Clearly,
candidates for judicial office were not intended to be included
in this provision of the law.

     We can only conclude that the absence of clear language
regarding the filing of SEIs for judicial candidates is an
oversight in the law which must be remedied through
legislation.  I have alerted our legislative coordinator to
this problem and have been assured that clarifying legislation
will be introduced as soon as is practicable.

     In the meantime, we recommend that you follow the same
requirements for candidates for judicial office as is currently
required for judges.  (Section 87500(i).)  This will allow the
general public to most easily review documents of individuals
competing for the same office, and is consistent with the
filing requirements for other publicly elected officials.

     Thank you for bringing this to our attention.  If you have
any questions, please call me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [illegible]
                                        By:  Lilly Spitz
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:LS:plh

SUPERIOR COURT/COUNTY CLERK                                    COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO

☒ 351 No. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor, San Bernardino, CA 92415-0210 • (714) 387-3921
☐ West District, 8303 Haven Avenue, Rancho Cucamonga, CA 91730 • (714) 945-4136
☐ Desert District, 14455 Civic Drive, Victorville, CA 92392 • (619) 243-8670
☐ North Desert District, 235 E. Mountain View Avenue, Barstow, CA 92311 • (619) 256-4816 98


April 6, 1988


Mr. Bruce Robeck
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Mr. Robeck:

Pursuant to our telephone conversation of April 1, 1988,
we are requesting a Letter of Interpretation for guidelines
as to where Candidate Statements' Forms 721's are to be
filed in San Bernardino County.

The Registrar receives all other campaign/election documents
but feels that the Forms 721's are to be filed with the
County Clerk's Office.  Enclosed is a copy of a transmittal
memo from the Registrar of Voters to the Technical
Assistance and Analysis Division on this subject.

The County Clerk's Office receives Forms 721 for the
Superior Court Judges, Commissioners, CAO, County Counsel,
District Attorney, Board of Supervisors and Planning
Commissioners.  After a review of our records for the past 3
years, we found no record of Candidate Statements being
received or filed in our office.

Therefore, a Letter of Interpretation is requested at this
time.

If you require further information, please contact me at
(714) 387-3970.

Sincerely,

Linda D. Hamilton

Linda D. Hamilton
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk

cc:Audrey Butler, Deputy Registrar

REGISTRAR OF VOTERS

COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDIN
GENERAL SERVICES AGENC

777 East Rialto Avenue • San Bernardino, CA 92415-0770 • (714) 387-8300

GERANE B. JACOBSEN
Registrar of Voters
F. P. HESTON
Assistant Registrar of Voters

March 28, 1988


TO: Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

FROM: REGISTRAR OF VOTERS, SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY
Audrey Butler, Deputy Registrar

RE: Candidates' Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721)

Confirming my telephone call with your office on March 14, and your
reply that information would be forthcoming in about a week with your
Memo on the handling of Form 721s. Your memo was received on
March 21 and we are forwarding 34 copies of Candidates' Form 721.

Your information that documents be date stamped was received after
the fact; also, inasmuch as the County Clerk is the filing officer,
we are also sending a copy there to be filed.

Please call A. Butler, 714/387-2082, if you have any questions or
if we can be of further service.

AB:mem

34 Enclosures: Forms 721

cc: Martin J. Moshier
County Clerk

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 21, 1988

Linda D. Hamilton
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk
351 No. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor
San Bernardino, CA 92415-0210

Re: File No. 88-141

Dear Ms. Hamilton:

Your letter received by the Fair Political Practices
Commission on April 8, 1988, has been reassigned to Lilly Spitz
an attorney in the Legal Division.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

MEMO                                                April 20, 1988

TO:     Diane

FROM:   Bruce [signature]

THRU:   Jeanne [signature]

RE:     Hamilton letter (I-88-141)

     I am returning the attached letter from Linda Hamilton because I
believe that the issue raised in the letter should be addressed by Legal
rather than by TAAA.  Briefly, the problem is that there is no statutory
basis for making an interpretation of the filing officer's responsibility
for statements of economic interest filed by candidates for judicial
office.

     Every candidate for an office enumerated in Section 87200 is required
to file a statement of economic interests.  (Section 87201.)  However,
unlike the provisions for other offices, no mention is made of where
candidates for judicial office should file their statements of economic
interests.  (Incumbents are noted at 87500(i).)

     The issues are:

          1.  Is the county clerk or the clerk of the court the officer
               who receives statements of economic interest for nonincumbent
               candidates for judicial office?

          2.  Is the filing official required to make and retain for public
               access a copy of the judicial candidates' statements of
               economic interest?


                                        P─
                                          Reassign to
                                        LS.  Thanks
                                          D.

. California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 11, 1988


Linda D. Hamilton
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk
351 No. Arrowhead Ave. 5th Floor
San Bernardino, CA  92415-0210

Re:  88-141

Dear Ms. Hamilton:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on April 8, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division
